LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TERRY W. FONG AND BETSY C. FONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART: THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANTS DID NOT OWE PLAINTIFF A DUTY TO PROTECT HIM FROM THIRD PARTY CRIMINAL ACTIVITY UNDER ANN M V. PACIFIC PLAZA SHOPPING CENTER (1993) 6 CAL. 4TH. 666 AND ITS PROGENY. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO DUTY UNDER DELGADO V. TRAX BAR & GRILL (2005) 36 CAL. 4TH. 224. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANTS DISCHARGED THEIR DUTY TO ACT REASONABLY AND CALL THE POLICE AS THE FIGHT UNFOLDED. (KUNDRA DECLARATION.). JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS TERRY W. FONG AND BETSY C. FONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for summary judgment is denied. Motion for summary adjudication is granted in part: The court finds that defendants did not owe plaintiff a duty to protect him from third party criminal activity under Ann M v. Pacific Plaza Shopping Center (1993) 6 Cal. 4th. 666 and its progeny. Summary Adjudication is denied as to duty under Delgado v. Trax Bar & Grill (2005) 36 Cal. 4th. 224. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendants discharged their duty to act reasonably and call the police as the fight unfolded. (Kundra declaration.). (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Discovery Order Pursuant To C.C.P. 2031.300 And C.C.P. 2023.030; And For Monetary Sanctions.
Off Calendar Dec-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
John Huster, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Jeremiah M. Cadigan

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Terry Fong's motion for terminating and monetary sanctions for failure to comply with prior order regarding requests for production of documents.
The Court has read and considered the late-filed opposition. The motion for terminating sanctions is denied. The motion for monetary sanctions is denied in light of the award of sanctions for the motion relating to non-compliance with the prior order regarding interrogatories. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Discovery Order Pursuantto C.C.P 2030.290 And C.C.P. 2023.030.
Off Calendar Dec-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
John Huster, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Jeremiah M. Cadigan

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Terry Fong's motion for terminating and monetary sanctions for failure to comply with prior order regarding interrogatories.
The Court has read and considered the late-filed opposition. Granted in part. The motion for terminating sanctions is denied. The motion for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $565, payable within 30 days of notice of this order. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
